title: Quantification of circulating microRNA using droplet digital PCR
author: Helle Glud Binderup
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.q8edzte

steps.instruction: Dual centrifugation use 10 ml of EDTA anticoagulated whole blood centrifugation at 3000 g for 15 minutes (acceleration 5, brake 6, temperature 18 °C) transfer plasma phase to new tube, leaving approximately 1 mL of plasma on top of the buffy coat centrifugation at 3000 g for 15 minutes (acceleration 5, brake 6, temperature 18 °C) transfer plasma phase to cryo tubes, leaving approximately 1 ml of plasma in the bottom of the tube store at -80 °C (Hettich centrifuge, 4706-01, Rotina 420R) Alternatively, a prolonged single centrifugation may be used 1. use 5 ml of EDTA anticoagulated whole blood 2. centrifuge at 3000 g for 30 minutes (acceleration 5, brake 6, temperature 18 °C) 3. transfer plasma phase to cryo-tubes, leaving approximately 0.5 ml of plasma on top of the buffy coat

Follow the instructions given by the manufacture (se notes before you start): a STEP 1: use 300 µL platelet-poor plasma STEP 4: after addition of isopropanol, add also 5 µL spike-in (cel-miR-39, 2.75 × 10 −12 M) STEP 6: perform the optional DNA digest
TaqMan®MicroRNA Reverse Transcription Kit Applied Biosystems (ThermoFisher Scientific) Catalog #4366597 20X TaqMan MicroRNA Assay Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #4440887

Prepare RT master mix: %s %s *add 10-20% excess volume **add water to a total reaction volume of 15 µL (including microRNA and RT-primers, see step 7) Mix gently and place on ice Remember to include a RT-negative sample (no template) It is also a good idea to include a RT-positive sample (a microRNA-sample included in all runs)

For each RT reaction, combine RT master mix with 2 µL of purified microRNA in a 0.2 µL polypropylene reaction tube (the volume of RT master mix is dependent on the number of RT-primers, see step 7) Keep on ice

Prepare RT primer mix: Add for each RT reaction 0.75 µL 20x RT primer from each microRNA assay set Example with two microRNA assays: %s %s Component Volume per 15 µL reaction* 20x RT specific primer #1 0.75 µL 20x RT specific primer #2 0.75 µL Total volume 1.5 µL** *add 10-20% excess volume **when using two microRNA assays the volume of water needed in step 5 is 8.66 µL for each reaction, and the volume of master mix added in step 6 is 11.5 µL Mix gently

steps.instruction: Add the appropiate volume of RT primer mix to the reaction tubes prepared in step 6 Mix gently and incubate on ice for 5 minutes
Transfer the reaction tubes to a termocycler Incubate at 16 °C for 30 min, at 42 °C for 30 min and at 85 °C for 5 min.
Continue immediately to the PCR amplification or store the RT-reaction in -20 °C.
20X TaqMan MicroRNA Assay Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #4440887 ddPCR Supermix for probes (no dUTP) Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863024
Prepare ddPCR reaction mix: %s %s *add 10-20% excess volume Mix gently
Dilute each of the RT-reactions (cDNAs) 1:10 with nucleace free water (e.g. 2 µL cDNA + 18 µL water) Combine 45.4 µL of ddPCR reaction mix with 2.6 µL of diluted RT-reaction (cDNA) in a 96 well plate
Transfer plate to termocycler Incubate in 95 °C for 5 minutes Proceed with 44 cycles of 95 °C for 15 sec and 60 °C for 60 sec. Final incubation in 98 ºC for 10 min Cool to 4 °C
ddPCR analysis is performed with QX100 Droplet Reader and QuantaSoft Software

DG32™ Automated Droplet Generator Cartridges Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1864108 Automated Droplet Generation Oil for Probes Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1864110 (AutoDG, 1864101, Automated Droplet Generator from BioRad)

Transfer 22 µL of the reaction mixture into each of two wells on an empty 96 well plate Heat sealing Transfer plate to the AutoDG The AutoDG transfers 20 µL from each well to a GD32 cartidge for droplet generation Heat sealing of output plate
Normalization is performed by calculating the relative concentration of target miRNA and cel-miR-39
